title: cistus creticus subsp. trabutii
intro: Cistus creticus subsp. trabutii is a subspecies of the rockrose plant, native to the Mediterranean region, known for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and digestive problems, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe and heal the skin. Cistus creticus subsp. trabutii can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or ointment, and is also used in traditional medicine as a treatment for colds, fever, and respiratory issues.
